content.text: This project is configured to connect to the ollama server specified in the .env. Can I configure it so it connects to my local ollama instance? the project will be dockerized and I am in nixos, will there be any problem?

displayContent.text:
# ─── Ports: Gateway ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRAEFIK_HTTP_PORT=12000
TRAEFIK_DASHBOARD_PORT=12001

# ─── Ports: Application Services ───────────────────────────────────────────
AUTH_SERVICE_PORT=12010
CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT=12011
ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT=12012
LANGGRAPH_PORT=12013
SCENARIO_WRITER_PORT=12016

# ─── Webapp API Mode ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VITE_API_MODE=real
VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:12000

# ─── Auth Domain DB ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AUTH_POSTGRES_USER=auth_user
AUTH_POSTGRES_PASSWORD=change_me_auth_db_password
AUTH_POSTGRES_DB=auth_db

# ─── AI Domain DB (LangGraph checkpoints) ──────────────────────────────────
LG_POSTGRES_USER=lg_user
LG_POSTGRES_PASSWORD=lg_dev_password
LG_POSTGRES_DB=lg_db
LANGGRAPH_POSTGRES_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://${LG_POSTGRES_USER}:${LG_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@langgraph-postgres:5432/${LG_POSTGRES_DB}

# ─── Artifacts Domain DB ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER=admin
ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD=artifacts_dev_password
ARTIFACTS_MONGO_DB=artifacts

# ─── Config Service Domain DB ──────────────────────────────────────────────
CONFIG_MONGO_USER=config_user
CONFIG_MONGO_PASSWORD=pipeline_dev_password
CONFIG_MONGO_DB=configurations

# ─── Scenario Writer Domain DB ─────────────────────────────────────────────
SCENARIO_MONGO_USER=scenario_user
SCENARIO_MONGO_PASSWORD=change_me_scenario_mongo_password
SCENARIO_MONGO_DB=scenarios

# ─── Auth & Secrets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
JWT_SECRET_KEY=e3541731ff9c13f5fadb902f9c155c40c31ae3cfb636aec6627966f77319f01b
INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN=dev-internal-token-change-in-prod
AUTH_SERVICE_URL=http://auth-service:12010

# ── Webapp / evaluation login credentials ─────────────────────────
BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL=admin@um.es
BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD=adminadmin

# ── LLM Provider ─────────────────────────────────────────────
AGENT_PROVIDER=ollama
USE_ENV_AGENTS=true

# Baseline benchmark model (litellm format, used by comparative baselines)
AGENT_MODEL=ollama/qwen3.6:35b

# Model names — orchestrator/planner tier vs worker/execution tier
AGENT_LARGE_MODEL=qwen3.6:35b
AGENT_SMALL_MODEL=glm-4.7-flash:q4_K_M

# Roles
AGENT_STRATEGIC_MODEL=qwen3.6:35b
AGENT_TACTICAL_MODEL=qwen3.6:35b
AGENT_WORKER_MODEL=glm-4.7-flash:q4_K_M
AGENT_VERIFIER_MODEL=glm-4.7-flash:q4_K_M

# DGX swarm via SSH tunnel on host port 11435
# (Containers reach the host via host.docker.internal, mapped in docker-compose extra_hosts)
OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434
OLLAMA_MODEL=qwen3.6:35b
OLLAMA_SMALL_MODEL=glm-4.7-flash:q4_K_M
OLLAMA_API_KEY=e6daa483a2b64ac487455000c8d5181e.lrCq4JkdkcuVEy_paLWg4QXR
OLLAMA_AUTO_PULL=false

# Scenario writer also needs the new URL
STORY_LLM_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434

# ─── Service URLs ───────────────────────
MONGODB_URL=mongodb://${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER}:${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD}@artifacts-mongo:27017/
MONGODB_DATABASE=${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_DB}
MCP_SERVER_URL=http://artifacts-service:${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT}/mcp

# ─── Concurrency ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Match OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL on the DGX to maximize KV cache utilisation
EVAL_CONCURRENCY=1      # 1 checklist at a time: enables Ollama KV-cache reuse across checklists sharing the same artifact context prefix
BENCHMARK_CONCURRENCY=2 # concurrent exercises for baseline benchmarks (set to 1 for full system)

# ─── Feature Flags ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SPECULATION_ENABLED=true
SPECULATION_THRESHOLD=0.8
PAUSE_BETWEEN_PHASES_DEFAULT=false
LAMINAR_ENABLED=false
RAG_ENABLED=false
OBSERVABILITY_BACKEND=console

58xn|# ─── Scenario Writer LLM ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
59za|# OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint for story generation.
60ot|# Defaults to the stack's Ollama instance; override for any remote provider.
61fb|STORY_LLM_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434
62qi|STORY_LLM_MODEL=qwen2.5:7b
63bn|STORY_LLM_API_KEY=

70pn|# ─── Image Generation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
71mh|# Set IMAGE_GEN_ENABLED=true and point IMAGE_GEN_BASE_URL at a server that
72js|# exposes POST /v1/images/generations (OpenAI Images API).

displayContent.text:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Co-Creation Framework — Environment Configuration
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Copy this to .env and fill in your actual values.
# NEVER commit .env to version control.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ─── Ports: Gateway ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRAEFIK_HTTP_PORT=12000
TRAEFIK_DASHBOARD_PORT=12001

# ─── Ports: Application Services ───────────────────────────────────────────
AUTH_SERVICE_PORT=12010
CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT=12011
ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT=12012
LANGGRAPH_PORT=12013
SCENARIO_WRITER_PORT=12016

# ─── Webapp API Mode ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Webapp API mode: "real" (Traefik, default) | "mock" (local fixtures, no backend needed)
VITE_API_MODE=real
# Base URL when VITE_API_MODE=real (must match Traefik port)
VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:12000

# ─── Ports: Dev DB Access (comment out entirely in production) ─────────────
MONGO_EXPRESS_PORT=12020
AUTH_POSTGRES_HOST_PORT=12021
LG_POSTGRES_HOST_PORT=12022
ARTIFACTS_MONGO_HOST_PORT=12023
CONFIG_MONGO_HOST_PORT=12024

# ─── Auth Domain DB ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AUTH_POSTGRES_USER=auth_user
AUTH_POSTGRES_PASSWORD=change_me_auth_db_password
AUTH_POSTGRES_DB=auth_db

# ─── AI Domain DB (LangGraph checkpoints) ──────────────────────────────────
LG_POSTGRES_USER=lg_user
LG_POSTGRES_PASSWORD=change_me_lg_db_password
LG_POSTGRES_DB=lg_db
LANGGRAPH_POSTGRES_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://${LG_POSTGRES_USER}:${LG_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@langgraph-postgres:5432/${LG_POSTGRES_DB}

# ─── Artifacts Domain DB ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER=admin
ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD=change_me_artifacts_mongo_password
ARTIFACTS_MONGO_DB=artifacts

# ─── Config Service Domain DB ──────────────────────────────────────────────
CONFIG_MONGO_USER=config_user
CONFIG_MONGO_PASSWORD=change_me_config_mongo_password
CONFIG_MONGO_DB=configurations

# ─── Scenario Writer Domain DB ─────────────────────────────────────────────
SCENARIO_MONGO_USER=scenario_user
SCENARIO_MONGO_PASSWORD=change_me_scenario_mongo_password
SCENARIO_MONGO_DB=scenarios

# ─── Scenario Writer LLM ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint for story generation.
# Defaults to the stack's Ollama instance; override for any remote provider.
STORY_LLM_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434
STORY_LLM_MODEL=qwen2.5:7b
STORY_LLM_API_KEY=
STORY_LLM_TEMPERATURE=0.85
STORY_LLM_MAX_TOKENS=8192
# "openai" = /v1/chat/completions (OpenAI, OpenRouter, Together.ai, vLLM …)
# "ollama" = native /api/chat (Ollama)
STORY_LLM_API_FORMAT=openai

# ─── Image Generation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Set IMAGE_GEN_ENABLED=true and point IMAGE_GEN_BASE_URL at a server that
# exposes POST /v1/images/generations (OpenAI Images API).
# Compatible with: Together.ai, fal.ai, Replicate, ComfyUI bridge, A1111, …
IMAGE_GEN_ENABLED=false
IMAGE_GEN_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:7860
IMAGE_GEN_MODEL=flux-dev
IMAGE_GEN_API_KEY=
IMAGE_GEN_WIDTH=1024
IMAGE_GEN_HEIGHT=1024
IMAGE_GEN_STEPS=25
IMAGE_GEN_TIMEOUT=120
# "b64_json" = embedded base64 in response | "url" = remote URL reference
IMAGE_GEN_RESPONSE_FORMAT=b64_json

# ─── Video Generation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Set VIDEO_GEN_ENABLED=true and configure the backend.
#
# Backends:
#   "runwayml"      — RunwayML Gen-3/Gen-4  (set VIDEO_GEN_BASE_URL=https://api.dev.runwayml.com)
#   "generic_async" — any server: POST /v1/videos/generations → {id}, GET /v1/videos/{id} → {status,url}
#   "generic_sync"  — simple server: POST /v1/videos/generations → {url}
VIDEO_GEN_ENABLED=false
VIDEO_GEN_BACKEND=runwayml
VIDEO_GEN_BASE_URL=https://api.dev.runwayml.com
VIDEO_GEN_MODEL=gen3a_turbo
VIDEO_GEN_API_KEY=
VIDEO_GEN_DURATION=5
VIDEO_GEN_WIDTH=1280
VIDEO_GEN_HEIGHT=720
VIDEO_GEN_TIMEOUT=300
VIDEO_GEN_POLL_INTERVAL=5

# ─── Auth & Secrets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# JWT secret for auth-service (minimum 32 characters)
# Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32
JWT_SECRET_KEY=change_me_in_production_minimum_32_characters

# Shared secret for internal service-to-service authentication (X-Service-Token header)
# Must be identical for all services that communicate internally
# Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32
INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN=change-me-in-production

# Internal URL of the auth service (used by config-service to resolve group memberships)
AUTH_SERVICE_URL=http://auth-service:12010

# Webapp / evaluation login credentials
# These are your application-level user credentials (Django user, not the DB user).
# Used by make benchmark-* targets to auto-login and obtain a JWT token.
# Also used as your day-to-day login at http://localhost:12000.
# Create the user once with: make up-webapp && docker exec auth-service python manage.py createsuperuser
BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL=
BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD=

# ── LLM Provider ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Options: ollama | vllm | openai | anthropic | google | openrouter
AGENT_PROVIDER=ollama

# Use environment-based agent configuration (true) or YAML files (false)
USE_ENV_AGENTS=true

# Model names (leave blank to use provider defaults)
# Ollama default: qwen3-coder-next:cloud
AGENT_LARGE_MODEL=
AGENT_SMALL_MODEL=

# Ollama base URL — set this to wherever your Ollama is running:
#   managed container (--profile managed-ollama): http://ollama:11434
#   Ollama on the same host as Docker            : http://host.docker.internal:11434
#   Remote server                                : http://<ip-or-hostname>:11434
# Do NOT append /v1 — the service handles API path construction internally.
OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434

# vLLM configuration (OpenAI-compatible)
# Base URL for vLLM server. Ensure /v1 is included as vLLM uses standard OpenAI paths.
VLLM_BASE_URL=http://vllm:8000/v1
# If running multiple models on one vLLM instance, specify them here.
# The adapter will use these to populate AGENT_LARGE_MODEL/AGENT_SMALL_MODEL if they are empty.
VLLM_MODEL=Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct
VLLM_SMALL_MODEL=Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-1.5B-Instruct
# Optional: API key if your vLLM gateway requires authentication
VLLM_API_KEY=
# Performance and memory tuning
VLLM_GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION=0.9
VLLM_MAX_MODEL_LEN=32768

# OpenAI / OpenRouter
OPENAI_API_KEY=
OPENROUTER_API_KEY=

# Anthropic
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=

# Google
GOOGLE_API_KEY=

# ─── Legacy LLM Configuration (used when USE_ENV_AGENTS=false) ─────────────
LLM_PROVIDER=gemini
LLM_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash
API_KEY=${GOOGLE_API_KEY}

# ─── Service URLs (internal, container-to-container) ───────────────────────
# These use container names as hostnames — do NOT change unless you know what you're doing
MONGODB_URL=mongodb://${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER}:${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD}@artifacts-mongo:27017/
MONGODB_DATABASE=${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_DB}
MCP_SERVER_URL=http://artifacts-service:${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT}/mcp
# Legacy evaluation backend URL (standalone evaluator scripts)
BACKEND_URL=http://backend:12101
RAG_SERVICE_URL=http://rag-backend:12200/api/v1

# ─── Feature Flags ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Speculative execution (autonomous mode)
SPECULATION_ENABLED=true
SPECULATION_THRESHOLD=0.8

# Pause between phases for human approval (autonomous mode)
PAUSE_BETWEEN_PHASES_DEFAULT=true

# Observability
LAMINAR_ENABLED=false
RAG_ENABLED=false

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# QUICK START EXAMPLES
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# To use Ollama locally:
# 1. Start Ollama: ollama serve
# 2. Pull models: ollama pull llama3.3 && ollama pull codestral
# 3. Set: AGENT_PROVIDER=ollama
# 4. Set: OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434   (no /v1 suffix)

# To use vLLM locally:
# 1. Start vLLM: vllm serve --model <model-name>
# 2. Set: AGENT_PROVIDER=vllm
# 3. Set: VLLM_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:8000/v1
# 4. To run 2 models on the same vLLM instance, provide their names in:
#    AGENT_LARGE_MODEL=my-large-model
#    AGENT_SMALL_MODEL=my-small-model
#    (Note: vLLM usually serves multiple models via the same /v1/chat/completions endpoint if they are loaded)

# To use Google Gemini:
# 1. Get API key from: https://makersuite.google.com/app/apikey
# 2. Set: AGENT_PROVIDER=gemini
# 3. Set: GOOGLE_API_KEY=your-key-here

# To use OpenRouter:
# 1. Get API key from: https://openrouter.ai/keys
# 2. Set: AGENT_PROVIDER=openrouter
# 3. Set: OPENROUTER_API_KEY=your-key-here

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT NOTES
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 1. Generate strong passwords for all database credentials
# 2. Use a 32+ character JWT_SECRET_KEY (openssl rand -hex 32)
# 3. Comment out ALL dev DB host port variables (12021-12024)
# 4. Do NOT run docker-compose with --profile dev in production
# 5. Store secrets in a secure vault (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, etc.)
# 6. Rotate all secrets regularly
# 7. Enable LAMINAR_ENABLED=true for production monitoring

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# See backend/agents/AGENT_CONFIG.md for detailed agent configuration guide
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

services:
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # DATABASE CONTAINERS — Each service owns its DB exclusively
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  auth-postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    container_name: auth-postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${AUTH_POSTGRES_USER:-auth_user}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${AUTH_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${AUTH_POSTGRES_DB:-auth_db}
    volumes:
      - auth_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${AUTH_POSTGRES_USER:-auth_user} -d ${AUTH_POSTGRES_DB:-auth_db}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    # NO ports here — exposed only under dev profile

  langgraph-postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    container_name: langgraph-postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${LG_POSTGRES_USER:-lg_user}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${LG_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${LG_POSTGRES_DB:-lg_db}
    volumes:
      - langgraph_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${LG_POSTGRES_USER:-lg_user} -d ${LG_POSTGRES_DB:-lg_db}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    # NO ports here — exposed only under dev profile

  artifacts-mongo:
    image: mongo:7.0
    container_name: artifacts-mongo
    command: ["mongod", "--quiet"]
    environment:
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: ${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER:-admin}
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD}
      MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE: ${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_DB:-artifacts}
    volumes:
      - artifacts_mongo_data:/data/db
      - artifacts_mongo_config:/data/configdb
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mongosh", "--norc", "--quiet", "--eval", "db.adminCommand('ping').ok"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 20s
    # NO ports here — exposed only under dev profile

  config-mongo:
    image: mongo:7.0
    container_name: config-mongo
    command: ["mongod", "--quiet"]
    environment:
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: ${CONFIG_MONGO_USER:-config_user}
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${CONFIG_MONGO_PASSWORD}
      MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE: ${CONFIG_MONGO_DB:-configurations}
    volumes:
      - config_mongo_data:/data/db
      - config_mongo_config:/data/configdb
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mongosh", "--norc", "--quiet", "--eval", "db.adminCommand('ping').ok"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 20s
    # NO ports here — exposed only under dev profile

  evaluation-mongo:
    image: mongo:7.0
    container_name: evaluation-mongo
    command: ["mongod", "--quiet"]
    environment:
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: ${EVAL_MONGO_USER:-eval_user}
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${EVAL_MONGO_PASSWORD:-eval_password}
      MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE: ${EVAL_MONGO_DB:-evaluations}
    volumes:
      - evaluation_mongo_data:/data/db
      - evaluation_mongo_config:/data/configdb
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mongosh", "--norc", "--quiet", "--eval", "db.adminCommand('ping').ok"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 20s
    # NO ports here — exposed only under dev profile

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # DEV TOOLS — Only for development
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  mongo-express:
    image: mongo-express:latest
    container_name: cc-mongo-express
    ports:
      - "${MONGO_EXPRESS_PORT:-12020}:8081"
    environment:
      - ME_CONFIG_MONGODB_ADMINUSERNAME=${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER:-admin}
      - ME_CONFIG_MONGODB_ADMINPASSWORD=${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD}
      - ME_CONFIG_MONGODB_URL=mongodb://${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER:-admin}:${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD}@artifacts-mongo:27017/
      - ME_CONFIG_BASICAUTH_USERNAME=admin
      - ME_CONFIG_BASICAUTH_PASSWORD=admin
      - ME_CONFIG_MONGODB_ENABLE_ADMIN=true
    depends_on:
      artifacts-mongo:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    profiles:
      - dev

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # LLM BACKEND — Managed Ollama Container
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  ollama:
    image: ollama/ollama:latest
    container_name: ollama
    profiles:
      - managed-ollama          # only starts when this profile is active
    entrypoint: ["/bin/bash", "/entrypoint.sh"]
    volumes:
      - ollama_data:/root/.ollama   # persists downloaded models
      - ./infrastructure/ollama/entrypoint.sh:/entrypoint.sh:ro
    environment:
      OLLAMA_AUTO_PULL: ${OLLAMA_AUTO_PULL:-true}
      OLLAMA_MODEL: ${OLLAMA_MODEL:-qwen2.5-coder:7b}
      # NO OLLAMA_API_KEY here — managed mode uses local models only
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:11434/api/tags"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 60s    # models may take time to initialise

  vllm:
    image: vllm/vllm-openai:latest
    container_name: vllm
    profiles:
      - vllm
    command: >
      --model ${VLLM_MODEL:-Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct}
      --gpu-memory-utilization ${VLLM_GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION:-0.9}
      --max-model-len ${VLLM_MAX_MODEL_LEN:-32768}
      --trust-remote-code
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    volumes:
      - ~/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface
    environment:
      - VLLM_API_KEY=${VLLM_API_KEY:-}
    deploy:
      resources:
        reservations:
          devices:
            - driver: nvidia
              count: all
              capabilities: [gpu]
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8000/v1/models"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 60s

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # API GATEWAY
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.6.1
    container_name: traefik
    command:
      - "--api.insecure=true"
      - "--providers.docker=true"
      - "--providers.docker.exposedbydefault=false"
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--log.level=INFO"
      - "--accesslog=true"
      - "--accesslog.format=json"
      - "--ping=true"
    ports:
      - "${TRAEFIK_HTTP_PORT:-12000}:80"
      - "${TRAEFIK_DASHBOARD_PORT:-12001}:8080"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--spider", "-q", "http://localhost:8080/ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # APPLICATION SERVICES
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  auth-service:
    build: ./auth-service
    container_name: auth-service
    depends_on:
      auth-postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010}:${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010}"
    environment:
      - PORT=${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010}
      - DB_HOST=auth-postgres
      - DB_USER=${AUTH_POSTGRES_USER:-auth_user}
      - DB_PASSWORD=${AUTH_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      - DB_NAME=${AUTH_POSTGRES_DB:-auth_db}
      - DB_PORT=5432
      - JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY}
      - DEBUG=True
      - ALLOWED_HOSTS=${AUTH_ALLOWED_HOSTS:-localhost,127.0.0.1,auth-service,traefik}
      - CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:12000,http://127.0.0.1:12000
      - CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:12000,http://127.0.0.1:12000
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live development
      - ./auth-service:/app
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.auth.rule=PathPrefix(`/api/v1/auth`) || PathPrefix(`/api/auth`) || PathPrefix(`/auth`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.auth.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.services.auth.loadbalancer.server.port=${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010}"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.cors.headers.accesscontrolallowmethods=GET,OPTIONS,PUT,POST,DELETE"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.cors.headers.accesscontrolallowheaders=*"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.cors.headers.accesscontrolalloworiginlist=http://localhost:12000,http://127.0.0.1:12000"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.cors.headers.accesscontrolmaxage=100"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.cors.headers.addvaryheader=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.auth.middlewares=cors"
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: >
      sh -c "python manage.py migrate &&
             python manage.py ensure_admin &&
             gunicorn config.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010} --workers 2"

  config-service:
    build: ./config-service
    container_name: config-service
    depends_on:
      config-mongo:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}:${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}"
    environment:
      - PORT=${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}
      - MONGODB_URL=mongodb://${CONFIG_MONGO_USER:-config_user}:${CONFIG_MONGO_PASSWORD}@config-mongo:27017/
      - MONGODB_DB=${CONFIG_MONGO_DB:-configurations}
      - JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY}
      - INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN=${INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN}
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.config-service.rule=PathPrefix(`/api/v1/metamodels`) || PathPrefix(`/api/v1/configurations`) || PathPrefix(`/api/v1/project-types`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.config-service.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.services.config-service.loadbalancer.server.port=${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}"
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./config-service/assets:/assets:ro
    command: >
      sh -c "uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}"

  artifacts-service:
    build: ./artifacts-service
    container_name: artifacts-service
    depends_on:
      artifacts-mongo:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}:${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}"
    environment:
      - PORT=${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}
      - MONGODB_URL=mongodb://${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER:-admin}:${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD}@artifacts-mongo:27017/
      - MONGODB_DB=${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_DB:-artifacts}
      - JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY}
      - INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN=${INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN}
      - LAMINAR_ENABLED=${LAMINAR_ENABLED:-false}
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.artifacts.rule=PathPrefix(`/api/v1/artifacts`) || PathPrefix(`/api/v1/topology`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.artifacts.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.services.artifacts.loadbalancer.server.port=${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}"
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./data:/data
      # Mount source code for live development
      - ./artifacts-service:/app
    command: >
      sh -c "uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}"

  evaluation-service:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: evaluation-service/Dockerfile
    container_name: evaluation-service
    depends_on:
      evaluation-mongo:
        condition: service_healthy
      artifacts-service:
        condition: service_started
    ports:
      - "${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}:${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}"
    environment:
      - PORT=${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}
      - MONGODB_URL=mongodb://${EVAL_MONGO_USER:-eval_user}:${EVAL_MONGO_PASSWORD:-eval_password}@evaluation-mongo:27017/
      - MONGODB_DB=${EVAL_MONGO_DB:-evaluations}
      - JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY}
      - INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN=${INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN}
      - ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_URL=http://artifacts-service:${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}
      - ORCHESTRATOR_URL=http://langgraph-orchestrator:${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}
      - OPENROUTER_API_KEY=${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - OPENAI_API_KEY=${OPENAI_API_KEY}
      - GOOGLE_API_KEY=${GOOGLE_API_KEY}
      - OLLAMA_BASE_URL=${OLLAMA_BASE_URL:-http://ollama:11434}
      - OLLAMA_API_KEY=${OLLAMA_API_KEY:-}
      - OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=${OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:-600}
      - APP_URL=${APP_URL:-http://localhost}
      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-INFO}
      # Concurrent checklist evaluations (match OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL on DGX)
      - EVAL_CONCURRENCY=${EVAL_CONCURRENCY:-4}
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:172.18.0.1"
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.evaluation.rule=PathPrefix(`/api/v1/evaluate`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.evaluation.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.services.evaluation.loadbalancer.server.port=${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}"
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      # Mount service code for live development
      - ./evaluation-service:/app/evaluation_service_src
      # Mount the evaluation framework so the service can import it
      - ./evaluation:/app/evaluation
    command: >
      sh -c "cd /app && uvicorn evaluation_service_src.app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}/health').read()\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 15s

  langgraph-orchestrator:
    build: ./langgraph-orchestrator
    container_name: langgraph-orchestrator
    # Note: In managed mode, start ollama service first with --profile managed-ollama
    # In external mode, the ollama service is not used - ensure your external
    # Ollama is running and reachable at OLLAMA_BASE_URL before starting.
    depends_on:
      langgraph-postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      artifacts-service:
        condition: service_started
      config-service:
        condition: service_started
      rag-service:
        condition: service_started
    ports:
      - "${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}:${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}"
    environment:
      - LANGGRAPH_PORT=${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}
      - HOST=0.0.0.0
      - LANGGRAPH_POSTGRES_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://${LG_POSTGRES_USER:-lg_user}:${LG_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@langgraph-postgres:5432/${LG_POSTGRES_DB:-lg_db}
      - ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_URL=http://artifacts-service:${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}
      - CONFIG_SERVICE_URL=http://config-service:${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}
      - AUTH_SERVICE_URL=http://auth-service:${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010}
      - JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY}
      - JWT_ALGORITHM=HS256
      - INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN=${INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN}
      - AGENT_PROVIDER=${AGENT_PROVIDER:-ollama}
      - AGENT_SMALL_MODEL=${AGENT_SMALL_MODEL}
      - AGENT_LARGE_MODEL=${AGENT_LARGE_MODEL}
      - AGENT_BASE_URL=${AGENT_BASE_URL}
      - AGENT_STRATEGIC_PROVIDER=${AGENT_STRATEGIC_PROVIDER:-ollama}
      - AGENT_STRATEGIC_MODEL=${AGENT_STRATEGIC_MODEL}
      - AGENT_STRATEGIC_BASE_URL=${AGENT_STRATEGIC_BASE_URL}
      - AGENT_STRATEGIC_TEMPERATURE=${AGENT_STRATEGIC_TEMPERATURE:-0.7}
      - AGENT_TACTICAL_PROVIDER=${AGENT_TACTICAL_PROVIDER:-ollama}
      - AGENT_TACTICAL_MODEL=${AGENT_TACTICAL_MODEL}
      - AGENT_TACTICAL_BASE_URL=${AGENT_TACTICAL_BASE_URL}
      - AGENT_TACTICAL_TEMPERATURE=${AGENT_TACTICAL_TEMPERATURE:-0.7}
      - AGENT_WORKER_PROVIDER=${AGENT_WORKER_PROVIDER:-ollama}
      - AGENT_WORKER_MODEL=${AGENT_WORKER_MODEL}
      - AGENT_WORKER_BASE_URL=${AGENT_WORKER_BASE_URL}
      - AGENT_WORKER_TEMPERATURE=${AGENT_WORKER_TEMPERATURE:-0.7}
      - AGENT_VERIFIER_PROVIDER=${AGENT_VERIFIER_PROVIDER:-ollama}
      - AGENT_VERIFIER_MODEL=${AGENT_VERIFIER_MODEL}
      - AGENT_VERIFIER_BASE_URL=${AGENT_VERIFIER_BASE_URL}
      - AGENT_VERIFIER_TEMPERATURE=${AGENT_VERIFIER_TEMPERATURE:-0.0}
      - OLLAMA_BASE_URL=${OLLAMA_BASE_URL:-http://ollama:11434}
      - OLLAMA_MODEL=${OLLAMA_MODEL:-qwen2.5-coder:7b}
      - OLLAMA_SMALL_MODEL=${OLLAMA_SMALL_MODEL:-${OLLAMA_MODEL:-qwen2.5-coder:7b}}
      - OLLAMA_API_KEY=${OLLAMA_API_KEY:-}
      - OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=${OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:-600}
      - OLLAMA_NUM_CTX=${OLLAMA_NUM_CTX:-32768}
      - OPENAI_API_KEY=${OPENAI_API_KEY}
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - GOOGLE_API_KEY=${GOOGLE_API_KEY}
      - OPENROUTER_API_KEY=${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}
      - SPECULATION_ENABLED=${SPECULATION_ENABLED:-true}
      - SPECULATION_THRESHOLD=${SPECULATION_THRESHOLD:-0.8}
      - PAUSE_BETWEEN_PHASES_DEFAULT=${PAUSE_BETWEEN_PHASES_DEFAULT:-true}
      - OBSERVABILITY_BACKEND=${OBSERVABILITY_BACKEND:-none}
      - LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY=${LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY:-}
      - LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=${LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY:-}
      - LANGFUSE_HOST=${LANGFUSE_HOST:-http://langfuse:3000}
      - EXPERIMENT_NAME=${EXPERIMENT_NAME:-default}
      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-INFO}
      - MCP_SERVER_URL=http://artifacts-service:${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}
      - RAG_ENABLED=${RAG_ENABLED:-true}
      - RAG_SERVICE_URL=http://rag-service:${RAG_SERVICE_PORT:-12014}
    extra_hosts:
      # Note: host-gateway doesn't work reliably with custom networks on Linux
      # So we hardcode the gateway IP of the co-creation-network (172.18.0.1)
      - "host.docker.internal:172.18.0.1"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live development (logs land in ./langgraph-orchestrator/logs/)
      - ./langgraph-orchestrator:/app
      # Docker socket for agentic sandbox (code execution)
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.orchestrator.rule=PathPrefix(`/api/v1/execute`) || PathPrefix(`/api/v1/runs`) || PathPrefix(`/api/v1/projects`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.orchestrator.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.routers.orchestrator.priority=100"
      - "traefik.http.services.orchestrator.loadbalancer.server.port=${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}"
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: >
      sh -c "python -m uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013} --reload"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}/health').read()\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 10s

  tui:
    build: ./tui
    container_name: cc-tui
    stdin_open: true
    tty: true
    environment:
      - CC_API_BASE_URL=http://traefik:80
      - TERM=xterm-256color
      - CC_TUI_TOKEN_FILE=/data/token
      - CC_TUI_LOG_FILE=/data/cc-tui.log
      - PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
    depends_on:
      - traefik
      - auth-service
    volumes:
      - ./tui:/app        # live source mount — changes apply on next `docker exec` session
      - ../assets:/assets        # live source mount — changes apply on next `docker exec` session
      - tui_data:/data
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    profiles:

displayContent.text:
services:
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # DATABASE CONTAINERS — Each service owns its DB exclusively
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  auth-postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    container_name: auth-postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${AUTH_POSTGRES_USER:-auth_user}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${AUTH_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${AUTH_POSTGRES_DB:-auth_db}
    volumes:
      - auth_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${AUTH_POSTGRES_USER:-auth_user} -d ${AUTH_POSTGRES_DB:-auth_db}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    # NO ports here — exposed only under dev profile

  langgraph-postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    container_name: langgraph-postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${LG_POSTGRES_USER:-lg_user}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${LG_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${LG_POSTGRES_DB:-lg_db}
    volumes:
      - langgraph_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${LG_POSTGRES_USER:-lg_user} -d ${LG_POSTGRES_DB:-lg_db}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    # NO ports here — exposed only under dev profile

  artifacts-mongo:
    image: mongo:7.0
    container_name: artifacts-mongo
    command: ["mongod", "--quiet"]
    environment:
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: ${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER:-admin}
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD}
      MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE: ${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_DB:-artifacts}
    volumes:
      - artifacts_mongo_data:/data/db
      - artifacts_mongo_config:/data/configdb
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mongosh", "--norc", "--quiet", "--eval", "db.adminCommand('ping').ok"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 20s
    # NO ports here — exposed only under dev profile

  config-mongo:
    image: mongo:7.0
    container_name: config-mongo
    command: ["mongod", "--quiet"]
    environment:
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: ${CONFIG_MONGO_USER:-config_user}
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${CONFIG_MONGO_PASSWORD}
      MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE: ${CONFIG_MONGO_DB:-configurations}
    volumes:
      - config_mongo_data:/data/db
      - config_mongo_config:/data/configdb
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mongosh", "--norc", "--quiet", "--eval", "db.adminCommand('ping').ok"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 20s
    # NO ports here — exposed only under dev profile

  evaluation-mongo:
    image: mongo:7.0
    container_name: evaluation-mongo
    command: ["mongod", "--quiet"]
    environment:
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: ${EVAL_MONGO_USER:-eval_user}
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${EVAL_MONGO_PASSWORD:-eval_password}
      MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE: ${EVAL_MONGO_DB:-evaluations}
    volumes:
      - evaluation_mongo_data:/data/db
      - evaluation_mongo_config:/data/configdb
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mongosh", "--norc", "--quiet", "--eval", "db.adminCommand('ping').ok"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 20s
    # NO ports here — exposed only under dev profile

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # DEV TOOLS — Only for development
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  mongo-express:
    image: mongo-express:latest
    container_name: cc-mongo-express
    ports:
      - "${MONGO_EXPRESS_PORT:-12020}:8081"
    environment:
      - ME_CONFIG_MONGODB_ADMINUSERNAME=${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER:-admin}
      - ME_CONFIG_MONGODB_ADMINPASSWORD=${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD}
      - ME_CONFIG_MONGODB_URL=mongodb://${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER:-admin}:${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD}@artifacts-mongo:27017/
      - ME_CONFIG_BASICAUTH_USERNAME=admin
      - ME_CONFIG_BASICAUTH_PASSWORD=admin
      - ME_CONFIG_MONGODB_ENABLE_ADMIN=true
    depends_on:
      artifacts-mongo:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    profiles:
      - dev

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # LLM BACKEND — Managed Ollama Container
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  ollama:
    image: ollama/ollama:latest
    container_name: ollama
    profiles:
      - managed-ollama          # only starts when this profile is active
    entrypoint: ["/bin/bash", "/entrypoint.sh"]
    volumes:
      - ollama_data:/root/.ollama   # persists downloaded models
      - ./infrastructure/ollama/entrypoint.sh:/entrypoint.sh:ro
    environment:
      OLLAMA_AUTO_PULL: ${OLLAMA_AUTO_PULL:-true}
      OLLAMA_MODEL: ${OLLAMA_MODEL:-qwen2.5-coder:7b}
      # NO OLLAMA_API_KEY here — managed mode uses local models only
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:11434/api/tags"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 60s    # models may take time to initialise

  vllm:
    image: vllm/vllm-openai:latest
    container_name: vllm
    profiles:
      - vllm
    command: >
      --model ${VLLM_MODEL:-Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct}
      --gpu-memory-utilization ${VLLM_GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION:-0.9}
      --max-model-len ${VLLM_MAX_MODEL_LEN:-32768}
      --trust-remote-code
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    volumes:
      - ~/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface
    environment:
      - VLLM_API_KEY=${VLLM_API_KEY:-}
    deploy:
      resources:
        reservations:
          devices:
            - driver: nvidia
              count: all
              capabilities: [gpu]
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8000/v1/models"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 60s

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # API GATEWAY
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.6.1
    container_name: traefik
    command:
      - "--api.insecure=true"
      - "--providers.docker=true"
      - "--providers.docker.exposedbydefault=false"
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--log.level=INFO"
      - "--accesslog=true"
      - "--accesslog.format=json"
      - "--ping=true"
    ports:
      - "${TRAEFIK_HTTP_PORT:-12000}:80"
      - "${TRAEFIK_DASHBOARD_PORT:-12001}:8080"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--spider", "-q", "http://localhost:8080/ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # APPLICATION SERVICES
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  auth-service:
    build: ./auth-service
    container_name: auth-service
    depends_on:
      auth-postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010}:${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010}"
    environment:
      - PORT=${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010}
      - DB_HOST=auth-postgres
      - DB_USER=${AUTH_POSTGRES_USER:-auth_user}
      - DB_PASSWORD=${AUTH_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      - DB_NAME=${AUTH_POSTGRES_DB:-auth_db}
      - DB_PORT=5432
      - JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY}
      - DEBUG=True
      - ALLOWED_HOSTS=${AUTH_ALLOWED_HOSTS:-localhost,127.0.0.1,auth-service,traefik}
      - CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:12000,http://127.0.0.1:12000
      - CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:12000,http://127.0.0.1:12000
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live development
      - ./auth-service:/app
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.auth.rule=PathPrefix(`/api/v1/auth`) || PathPrefix(`/api/auth`) || PathPrefix(`/auth`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.auth.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.services.auth.loadbalancer.server.port=${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010}"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.cors.headers.accesscontrolallowmethods=GET,OPTIONS,PUT,POST,DELETE"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.cors.headers.accesscontrolallowheaders=*"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.cors.headers.accesscontrolalloworiginlist=http://localhost:12000,http://127.0.0.1:12000"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.cors.headers.accesscontrolmaxage=100"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.cors.headers.addvaryheader=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.auth.middlewares=cors"
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: >
      sh -c "python manage.py migrate &&
             python manage.py ensure_admin &&
             gunicorn config.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010} --workers 2"

  config-service:
    build: ./config-service
    container_name: config-service
    depends_on:
      config-mongo:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}:${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}"
    environment:
      - PORT=${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}
      - MONGODB_URL=mongodb://${CONFIG_MONGO_USER:-config_user}:${CONFIG_MONGO_PASSWORD}@config-mongo:27017/
      - MONGODB_DB=${CONFIG_MONGO_DB:-configurations}
      - JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY}
      - INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN=${INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN}
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.config-service.rule=PathPrefix(`/api/v1/metamodels`) || PathPrefix(`/api/v1/configurations`) || PathPrefix(`/api/v1/project-types`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.config-service.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.services.config-service.loadbalancer.server.port=${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}"
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./config-service/assets:/assets:ro
    command: >
      sh -c "uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}"

  artifacts-service:
    build: ./artifacts-service
    container_name: artifacts-service
    depends_on:
      artifacts-mongo:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}:${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}"
    environment:
      - PORT=${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}
      - MONGODB_URL=mongodb://${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_USER:-admin}:${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_PASSWORD}@artifacts-mongo:27017/
      - MONGODB_DB=${ARTIFACTS_MONGO_DB:-artifacts}
      - JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY}
      - INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN=${INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN}
      - LAMINAR_ENABLED=${LAMINAR_ENABLED:-false}
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.artifacts.rule=PathPrefix(`/api/v1/artifacts`) || PathPrefix(`/api/v1/topology`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.artifacts.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.services.artifacts.loadbalancer.server.port=${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}"
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./data:/data
      # Mount source code for live development
      - ./artifacts-service:/app
    command: >
      sh -c "uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}"

  evaluation-service:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: evaluation-service/Dockerfile
    container_name: evaluation-service
    depends_on:
      evaluation-mongo:
        condition: service_healthy
      artifacts-service:
        condition: service_started
    ports:
      - "${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}:${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}"
    environment:
      - PORT=${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}
      - MONGODB_URL=mongodb://${EVAL_MONGO_USER:-eval_user}:${EVAL_MONGO_PASSWORD:-eval_password}@evaluation-mongo:27017/
      - MONGODB_DB=${EVAL_MONGO_DB:-evaluations}
      - JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY}
      - INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN=${INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN}
      - ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_URL=http://artifacts-service:${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}
      - ORCHESTRATOR_URL=http://langgraph-orchestrator:${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}
      - OPENROUTER_API_KEY=${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - OPENAI_API_KEY=${OPENAI_API_KEY}
      - GOOGLE_API_KEY=${GOOGLE_API_KEY}
      - OLLAMA_BASE_URL=${OLLAMA_BASE_URL:-http://ollama:11434}
      - OLLAMA_API_KEY=${OLLAMA_API_KEY:-}
      - OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=${OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:-600}
      - APP_URL=${APP_URL:-http://localhost}
      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-INFO}
      # Concurrent checklist evaluations (match OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL on DGX)
      - EVAL_CONCURRENCY=${EVAL_CONCURRENCY:-4}
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:172.18.0.1"
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.evaluation.rule=PathPrefix(`/api/v1/evaluate`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.evaluation.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.services.evaluation.loadbalancer.server.port=${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}"
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      # Mount service code for live development
      - ./evaluation-service:/app/evaluation_service_src
      # Mount the evaluation framework so the service can import it
      - ./evaluation:/app/evaluation
    command: >
      sh -c "cd /app && uvicorn evaluation_service_src.app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:${EVALUATION_SERVICE_PORT:-12015}/health').read()\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 15s

  langgraph-orchestrator:
    build: ./langgraph-orchestrator
    container_name: langgraph-orchestrator
    # Note: In managed mode, start ollama service first with --profile managed-ollama
    # In external mode, the ollama service is not used - ensure your external
    # Ollama is running and reachable at OLLAMA_BASE_URL before starting.
    depends_on:
      langgraph-postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      artifacts-service:
        condition: service_started
      config-service:
        condition: service_started
      rag-service:
        condition: service_started
    ports:
      - "${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}:${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}"
    environment:
      - LANGGRAPH_PORT=${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}
      - HOST=0.0.0.0
      - LANGGRAPH_POSTGRES_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://${LG_POSTGRES_USER:-lg_user}:${LG_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@langgraph-postgres:5432/${LG_POSTGRES_DB:-lg_db}
      - ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_URL=http://artifacts-service:${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}
      - CONFIG_SERVICE_URL=http://config-service:${CONFIG_SERVICE_PORT:-12011}
      - AUTH_SERVICE_URL=http://auth-service:${AUTH_SERVICE_PORT:-12010}
      - JWT_SECRET_KEY=${JWT_SECRET_KEY}
      - JWT_ALGORITHM=HS256
      - INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN=${INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN}
      - AGENT_PROVIDER=${AGENT_PROVIDER:-ollama}
      - AGENT_SMALL_MODEL=${AGENT_SMALL_MODEL}
      - AGENT_LARGE_MODEL=${AGENT_LARGE_MODEL}
      - AGENT_BASE_URL=${AGENT_BASE_URL}
      - AGENT_STRATEGIC_PROVIDER=${AGENT_STRATEGIC_PROVIDER:-ollama}
      - AGENT_STRATEGIC_MODEL=${AGENT_STRATEGIC_MODEL}
      - AGENT_STRATEGIC_BASE_URL=${AGENT_STRATEGIC_BASE_URL}
      - AGENT_STRATEGIC_TEMPERATURE=${AGENT_STRATEGIC_TEMPERATURE:-0.7}
      - AGENT_TACTICAL_PROVIDER=${AGENT_TACTICAL_PROVIDER:-ollama}
      - AGENT_TACTICAL_MODEL=${AGENT_TACTICAL_MODEL}
      - AGENT_TACTICAL_BASE_URL=${AGENT_TACTICAL_BASE_URL}
      - AGENT_TACTICAL_TEMPERATURE=${AGENT_TACTICAL_TEMPERATURE:-0.7}
      - AGENT_WORKER_PROVIDER=${AGENT_WORKER_PROVIDER:-ollama}
      - AGENT_WORKER_MODEL=${AGENT_WORKER_MODEL}
      - AGENT_WORKER_BASE_URL=${AGENT_WORKER_BASE_URL}
      - AGENT_WORKER_TEMPERATURE=${AGENT_WORKER_TEMPERATURE:-0.7}
      - AGENT_VERIFIER_PROVIDER=${AGENT_VERIFIER_PROVIDER:-ollama}
      - AGENT_VERIFIER_MODEL=${AGENT_VERIFIER_MODEL}
      - AGENT_VERIFIER_BASE_URL=${AGENT_VERIFIER_BASE_URL}
      - AGENT_VERIFIER_TEMPERATURE=${AGENT_VERIFIER_TEMPERATURE:-0.0}
      - OLLAMA_BASE_URL=${OLLAMA_BASE_URL:-http://ollama:11434}
      - OLLAMA_MODEL=${OLLAMA_MODEL:-qwen2.5-coder:7b}
      - OLLAMA_SMALL_MODEL=${OLLAMA_SMALL_MODEL:-${OLLAMA_MODEL:-qwen2.5-coder:7b}}
      - OLLAMA_API_KEY=${OLLAMA_API_KEY:-}
      - OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=${OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:-600}
      - OLLAMA_NUM_CTX=${OLLAMA_NUM_CTX:-32768}
      - OPENAI_API_KEY=${OPENAI_API_KEY}
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - GOOGLE_API_KEY=${GOOGLE_API_KEY}
      - OPENROUTER_API_KEY=${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}
      - SPECULATION_ENABLED=${SPECULATION_ENABLED:-true}
      - SPECULATION_THRESHOLD=${SPECULATION_THRESHOLD:-0.8}
      - PAUSE_BETWEEN_PHASES_DEFAULT=${PAUSE_BETWEEN_PHASES_DEFAULT:-true}
      - OBSERVABILITY_BACKEND=${OBSERVABILITY_BACKEND:-none}
      - LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY=${LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY:-}
      - LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=${LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY:-}
      - LANGFUSE_HOST=${LANGFUSE_HOST:-http://langfuse:3000}
      - EXPERIMENT_NAME=${EXPERIMENT_NAME:-default}
      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-INFO}
      - MCP_SERVER_URL=http://artifacts-service:${ARTIFACTS_SERVICE_PORT:-12012}
      - RAG_ENABLED=${RAG_ENABLED:-true}
      - RAG_SERVICE_URL=http://rag-service:${RAG_SERVICE_PORT:-12014}
    extra_hosts:
      # Note: host-gateway doesn't work reliably with custom networks on Linux
      # So we hardcode the gateway IP of the co-creation-network (172.18.0.1)
      - "host.docker.internal:172.18.0.1"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live development (logs land in ./langgraph-orchestrator/logs/)
      - ./langgraph-orchestrator:/app
      # Docker socket for agentic sandbox (code execution)
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.orchestrator.rule=PathPrefix(`/api/v1/execute`) || PathPrefix(`/api/v1/runs`) || PathPrefix(`/api/v1/projects`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.orchestrator.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.routers.orchestrator.priority=100"
      - "traefik.http.services.orchestrator.loadbalancer.server.port=${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}"
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: >
      sh -c "python -m uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013} --reload"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:${LANGGRAPH_PORT:-12013}/health').read()\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 10s

  tui:
    build: ./tui
    container_name: cc-tui
    stdin_open: true
    tty: true
    environment:
      - CC_API_BASE_URL=http://traefik:80
      - TERM=xterm-256color
      - CC_TUI_TOKEN_FILE=/data/token
      - CC_TUI_LOG_FILE=/data/cc-tui.log
      - PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
    depends_on:
      - traefik
      - auth-service
    volumes:
      - ./tui:/app        # live source mount — changes apply on next `docker exec` session
      - ../assets:/assets        # live source mount — changes apply on next `docker exec` session
      - tui_data:/data
    networks:
      - co-creation-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    profiles:

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. The project is already configured to use a local Ollama instance. The key configuration is in `.env`:

- `OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434` — this is the current setting, pointing to the host machine's Ollama
- `STORY_LLM_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434` — same for the scenario writer

The user asks two things:
1. Can they configure it to connect to their local Ollama instance?
2. They're on NixOS and the project will be Dockerized — will there be problems?

Let me check the docker-compose for `extra_hosts` mappings, which are critical for `host.docker.internal` to work.

Looking at the compose file, I see:
- `langgraph-orchestrator` has `extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:172.18.0.1"`
- `evaluation-service` has `extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:172.18.0.1"`

The problem is that `host.docker.internal` is hardcoded to `172.18.0.1`, which is the gateway IP of the Docker network. This may or may not work depending on the Docker network configuration.

On NixOS, there are specific considerations:
1. NixOS uses systemd and has its own way of configuring Docker
2. `host.docker.internal` on Linux doesn't work the same as on macOS/Windows — Docker on Linux doesn't automatically provide `host.docker.internal`
3. The hardcoded `172.18.0.1` IP is fragile — it depends on the Docker network's subnet allocation

Let me also check if there's a `DOCKER_DAEMON` or any NixOS-specific config. Let me also look at how the services that need Ollama are configured.

The current `.env` already has:
```
OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434
```

So it's already configured for a "local" Ollama. The question is really about whether `host.docker.internal` works on NixOS with Docker.

Let me check the rest of the docker-compose to see if there's a network definition with a specific subnet.

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me also check `.env.template` for the NixOS-specific notes and the `llm_adapter.py` for the connectivity check.

The key points:

1. **Current `.env` already points to `host.docker.internal:11434`** — this is the correct configuration for connecting to a local Ollama instance from Docker containers.

2. **NixOS concerns:**
   - On Linux (including NixOS), Docker doesn't provide `host.docker.internal` automatically like it does on macOS/Windows. The project works around this with `extra_hosts` mappings in `docker-compose.yml`, hardcoding `host.docker.internal` to `172.18.0.1` (the Docker network gateway).
   - However, `172.18.0.1` is a **hardcoded assumption** about the Docker network subnet. If the `co-creation-network` bridge gets assigned a different subnet, this breaks.
   - NixOS runs Docker via virtualisation.docker or services.docker, which should work fine, but the `host.docker.internal` mapping needs to be correct.

3. **The `.env.template` already has a note about NixOS** (line 81-82): "Fallback on NixOS / Linux if host.docker.internal fails: OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://172.17.0.1:11434"

Let me check the network definition more carefully and also the llm_adapter.py for the connectivity check logic.

content.text:
Your project is already configured for local Ollama — the `.env` has `OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434`. The real question is whether that hostname resolves correctly from inside Docker containers on NixOS. There are two problems:

### Problem 1: `host.docker.internal` on Linux

Docker on Linux (including NixOS) does **not** automatically provide `host.docker.internal` like macOS/Windows Docker Desktop does. The project works around this by hardcoding it in `docker-compose.yml`:

```yaml
extra_hosts:
  - "host.docker.internal:172.18.0.1"
```

This is applied to `evaluation-service`, `langgraph-orchestrator`, and `benchmark` services. The IP `172.18.0.1` is the assumed gateway of the `co-creation-network` bridge — but the network definition doesn't pin the subnet:

```yaml
networks:
  co-creation-network:
    driver: bridge
    # no ipam config → subnet is dynamically assigned
```

Docker can assign **any** subnet to that network. If it picks `172.19.0.0/16` instead of `172.18.0.0/16`, the hardcoded `172.18.0.1` points to nothing and Ollama becomes unreachable.

### Problem 2: Ollama must listen on `0.0.0.0`

By default, Ollama on Linux binds to `127.0.0.1:11434`. Docker containers reaching via `host.docker.internal` hit the host's external interface, not loopback. If Ollama only listens on localhost, the connection is refused regardless of routing.

### Fixes

**1. Pin the Docker network subnet** so `172.18.0.1` is guaranteed:

```yaml
networks:
  co-creation-network:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: 172.18.0.0/16
          gateway: 172.18.0.1
```

**2. Make Ollama listen on all interfaces** on NixOS. Add to your NixOS configuration:

```nix
services.ollama = {
  enable = true;
  host = "0.0.0.0";   # or set OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0 in environment
};
```

Or if you run Ollama outside NixOS service management, start it with:
```bash
OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0 ollama serve
```

**3. Verify** after `make up`:
```bash
# From inside a container:
docker exec langgraph-orchestrator python -c "import urllib.request; print(urllib.request.urlopen('http://host.docker.internal:11434/api/tags').read())"

# From the host:
curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags
```

### NixOS-specific notes

- The NixOS Docker module (`virtualisation.docker`) works fine; just make sure the daemon is enabled: `virtualisation.docker.enable = true`.
- Your user must be in the `docker` group, or you'll need `sudo` for every `docker`/`docker-compose` command.
- The `.env.template` mentions a fallback of `http://172.17.0.1:11434` — that's the Docker default bridge gateway. It works **only** if `co-creation-network` isn't used or happens to share that subnet. It's not reliable with custom networks. Pinning the subnet (fix 1) is the correct approach.

displayContent.text:
#!/bin/bash
# experiment_launcher.sh — Interactive Experiment Manager
#
# A definitive launcher for co-creation experiments. 
# Features:
#   - Multi-dataset selection with shortcuts (all, creation, modify)
#   - Persistent Experiment Name (for multi-day runs)
#   - Interactive exercise selection (multi-select)
#   - Target suite selection (Full, Baseline, Ablation, All)
#   - Mode selection (Create, Judge, Both)
#   - Force flags (Force Create, Force Judge)
#   - Statistics & Progress tracking

set -euo pipefail

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEFAULT_RUN_NAME="tfm_experiment"
RUN_NAME="${RUN_NAME:-$DEFAULT_RUN_NAME}"
RESULTS_DIR="evaluation/results/${RUN_NAME}"
TEMP_DATASET=".tmp_combined_dataset.json"
TEMP_INPUT=".tmp_launcher_input.json"

# Colors for UI
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
RED='\033[0;31m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_header() {
    clear
    echo -e "${BLUE}================================================================${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE}        🚀 CO-CREATION FRAMEWORK | EXPERIMENT LAUNCHER        ${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE}================================================================${NC}"
    echo -e "Current Run Name: ${GREEN}${RUN_NAME}${NC}"
    echo -e "Results Path:     ${GREEN}${RESULTS_DIR}${NC}"
    echo ""
}

_check_dependencies() {
    if ! command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
        echo -e "${RED}Error: 'jq' is not installed. Please install it to use the launcher.${NC}"
        exit 1
    fi
}

_choose_run_name() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Step 1: Confirm Experiment (Run) Name${NC}"
    echo -e "Existing runs share the same name to accumulate results."
    read -p "Enter Run Name [default: $RUN_NAME]: " input_name
    if [[ -n "$input_name" ]]; then
        RUN_NAME="$input_name"
        RESULTS_DIR="evaluation/results/${RUN_NAME}"
    fi
    echo ""
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Statistics & Status Table
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_show_stats() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}--- Current Progress in '${RUN_NAME}' ---${NC}"
    if [[ ! -d "$RESULTS_DIR" ]]; then
        echo "No results yet for this run directory."
        echo ""
        return
    fi

    python3 - "$RESULTS_DIR" <<'PYEOF'
import json, os, sys
from pathlib import Path

res_dir = Path(sys.argv[1])
targets = [d for d in res_dir.iterdir() if d.is_dir()]

print(f"{'Target':<22} | {'OK':>4} | {'Valid':>6} | {'Score':>6}")
print("-" * 50)

for t in sorted(targets):
    res_file = t / "results.json"
    if not res_file.exists(): continue
    try:
        data = json.load(open(res_file))
        results = data.get("creation", {}).get("results", [])
        completed = [r for r in results if r.get("status") == "completed"]
        
        valid_rates = []
        scores = []
        for r in completed:
            v = r.get("detailed_metrics", {}).get("validation", {})
            if v.get("total", 0) > 0:
                valid_rates.append(v["valid"] / v["total"])
            
            s = r.get("evaluation", {}).get("overall_score")
            if s is not None: scores.append(s)

        avg_v = sum(valid_rates)/len(valid_rates) if valid_rates else 0
        avg_s = sum(scores)/len(scores) if scores else 0
        
        print(f"{t.name:<22} | {len(completed):>4} | {avg_v:>5.0%} | {avg_s:>5.0%}")
    except:
        print(f"{t.name:<22} | Error reading results.json")
PYEOF
    echo ""
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Menu: Dataset Discovery & Selection
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_choose_input_file() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Step 2: Select Input Dataset(s)${NC}"
    echo -e "Options: numbers (e.g. '1 2 5'), 'all', 'creation' (ex_*), 'modify' (mod_*)"
    
    # Discovery (root datasets + smoke)
    mapfile -t datasets < <(ls evaluation/datasets/*.json evaluation/datasets/smoke/*.json 2>/dev/null | sort -u)
    
    for i in "${!datasets[@]}"; do
        echo -e "  $((i+1))) ${datasets[$i]}"
    done
    
    read -p "Selection: " ds_choice
    
    # Reset temp combined dataset
    echo "[]" > "$TEMP_DATASET"
    SELECTED_FILES=()

    if [[ "$ds_choice" == "all" ]]; then
        SELECTED_FILES=("${datasets[@]}")
    elif [[ "$ds_choice" == "creation" ]]; then
        for f in "${datasets[@]}"; do [[ "$f" == *"ex_"* || "$f" == *"creation"* ]] && SELECTED_FILES+=("$f"); done
    elif [[ "$ds_choice" == "modify" ]]; then
        for f in "${datasets[@]}"; do [[ "$f" == *"mod_"* || "$f" == *"modify"* ]] && SELECTED_FILES+=("$f"); done
    else
        for idx in $ds_choice; do
            real_idx=$((idx-1))
            if [[ $real_idx -ge 0 && $real_idx -lt ${#datasets[@]} ]]; then
                SELECTED_FILES+=("${datasets[$real_idx]}")
            fi
        done
    fi

    if [[ ${#SELECTED_FILES[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo -e "${RED}No datasets selected. Exiting.${NC}"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Merge selected JSON files
    echo -e "Merging ${#SELECTED_FILES[@]} datasets..."
    for f in "${SELECTED_FILES[@]}"; do
        # Extract the array (whether it's raw or under an 'exercises' key) and merge
        jq -s 'map(if type == "array" then . else .exercises end) | add' "$TEMP_DATASET" "$f" > "$TEMP_DATASET.tmp" && mv "$TEMP_DATASET.tmp" "$TEMP_DATASET"
    done
    echo ""
}

_choose_exercises() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Step 3: Select Exercises to Run${NC}"
    echo -e "(Enter numbers separated by space, e.g. '1 3 5', or type 'all')"
    
    # Extract IDs and Names from the combined dataset
    mapfile -t exercise_list < <(jq -r '.[] | "\(.id // .ID): \(.name // .Name)"' "$TEMP_DATASET")
    
    for i in "${!exercise_list[@]}"; do
        echo -e "  $((i+1))) ${exercise_list[$i]}"
    done
    
    read -p "Selection: " choice
    
    if [[ "$choice" == "all" ]]; then
        cp "$TEMP_DATASET" "$TEMP_INPUT"
        SELECTED_COUNT=$(jq '. | length' "$TEMP_INPUT")
    else
        echo "[]" > "$TEMP_INPUT"
        for idx in $choice; do
            real_idx=$((idx-1))
            if [[ $real_idx -ge 0 && $real_idx -lt ${#exercise_list[@]} ]]; then
                id=$(echo "${exercise_list[$real_idx]}" | cut -d: -f1)
                jq --arg id "$id" '. + [input[] | select((.id // .ID) == $id)]' "$TEMP_INPUT" "$TEMP_DATASET" > "$TEMP_INPUT.tmp" && mv "$TEMP_INPUT.tmp" "$TEMP_INPUT"
            fi
        done
        SELECTED_COUNT=$(jq '. | length' "$TEMP_INPUT")
    fi
    echo -e "Selected ${GREEN}${SELECTED_COUNT}${NC} exercises."
    echo ""
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Menu: Target Suites
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_choose_suite() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Step 4: Select Target Suite${NC}"
    options=("full" "baselines" "only-baselines" "ablation" "only-ablation" "modify" "all")
    select SUITE in "${options[@]}"; do
        if [[ -n "$SUITE" ]]; then
            break
        fi
    done
    echo ""
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Menu: Mode & Flags
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_choose_mode() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Step 5: Select Mode & Flags${NC}"
    echo "  1) Run Everything (Create + Validate + Judge)"
    echo "  2) Create Only (Stop before validation)"
    echo "  3) Judge Only (Use existing snapshots)"
    read -p "Choice [1-3]: " mode_choice
    
    ONLY_JUDGE=0
    SKIP_EVALUATE=0
    case $mode_choice in
        2) SKIP_EVALUATE=1 ;;
        3) ONLY_JUDGE=1 ;;
    esac
    
    echo -e "\n${YELLOW}Options:${NC}"
    FORCE_CREATE=0
    FORCE_JUDGE=0
    
    read -p "Force Re-Creation? (y/N): " fc
    [[ "$fc" == "y" || "$fc" == "Y" ]] && FORCE_CREATE=1
    
    read -p "Force Re-Judgment? (y/N): " fj
    [[ "$fj" == "y" || "$fj" == "Y" ]] && FORCE_JUDGE=1
    
    echo ""
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Execution
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_confirm_and_run() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}Configuration Summary:${NC}"
    echo -e "  Run Name: ${GREEN}${RUN_NAME}${NC}"
    echo -e "  Count:    ${SELECTED_COUNT} exercises"
    echo -e "  Suite:    ${SUITE}"
    echo -e "  Mode:     $( [[ $ONLY_JUDGE -eq 1 ]] && echo "Judge Only" || ( [[ $SKIP_EVALUATE -eq 1 ]] && echo "Create Only" || echo "Full (Create + Judge)" ) )"
    echo -e "  Force:    Create=$FORCE_CREATE, Judge=$FORCE_JUDGE"
    echo ""
    read -p "Launch experiment? (y/N): " confirm
    [[ "$confirm" != "y" && "$confirm" != "Y" ]] && exit 0

    _header
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Launching...${NC}"

    # Construct make command
    CMD="make benchmark-study RUN_NAME=\"$RUN_NAME\" SUITE=\"$SUITE\" BENCHMARK_INPUT=\"$TEMP_INPUT\""
    
    [[ $ONLY_JUDGE -eq 1 ]]    && CMD="$CMD ONLY_JUDGE=1"
    [[ $SKIP_EVALUATE -eq 1 ]] && CMD="$CMD SKIP_EVALUATE=1"
    [[ $FORCE_CREATE -eq 1 ]]  && CMD="$CMD FORCE_CREATE=1"
    [[ $FORCE_JUDGE -eq 1 ]]   && CMD="$CMD FORCE_JUDGE=1"

    echo -e "Executing: ${BLUE}$CMD${NC}\n"
    eval "$CMD"
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entry Point
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_check_dependencies
_header
_choose_run_name
_show_stats
_choose_input_file
_choose_exercises
_choose_suite
_choose_mode
_confirm_and_run

# Cleanup
rm -f "$TEMP_DATASET" "$TEMP_INPUT"

5qn|# ==============================================================================

10wf|#   AUTH_TOKEN              pre-obtained JWT (optional — login is automatic)

69ok|# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

79vm|else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-baselines)

84ni|_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_ABLATION)

97jo|# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
98fd|# Shared auth macro — acquires a JWT and exports it as $$TOKEN.
99vs|# Uses AUTH_TOKEN directly if provided; otherwise logs in from .env credentials.
100sb|# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101wx|define _GET_TOKEN
102lr|TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
103ql|if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
104lb|	if [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" ] || [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" ]; then \
105lb|		echo "ERROR: BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL or BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD not set in .env"; \
106ak|		exit 1; \
107px|	fi; \
108sd|	echo "  Attempting auth for $(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)..."; \
109ru|	RESPONSE=$$(curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12010/api/auth/login/" \
110cf|		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
111aa|		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}" \
112pc|		|| curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12000/api/v1/auth/login/" \
113cf|		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
114ct|		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}"); \
115qi|	TOKEN=$$(echo "$$RESPONSE" | $(PYTHON) -c "import sys, json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('access', ''))" 2>/dev/null); \
116vm|	if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
117ra|		echo "ERROR: Auth failed. Response from server:"; \
118fu|		echo "$$RESPONSE"; \
119ak|		exit 1; \
120px|	fi; \
121ya|	echo "  Auth OK"; \
122og|fi
123sx|endef
124th|
125hy|# Same macro for inside a running Docker container (uses internal hostnames)
126rp|define _GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER
127lr|TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
128ql|if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
129dr|	TOKEN=$$(docker exec benchmark-env python3 -c \
130dr|		"import httpx; r=httpx.post('http://auth-service:12010/api/auth/login/', \
131rw|		    json={'email':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)','password':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)'}, \
132ky|		    timeout=20); r.raise_for_status(); print(r.json()['access'])"); \
133pm|	echo "  Auth OK inside container ($(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL))"; \
134og|fi
135sx|endef

163he|	@echo "                                    Loops over all ex_*.json datasets."
164bz|	@echo "    SUITE=baselines|ablation|modify|smoke|full|all  (default: baselines)"
165dz|	@echo "    DATASET=path/to/ex_N.json          run a single dataset instead"

169mr|	@echo "    BENCHMARK_INPUT=...  RUN_NAME=... SUITE=..."

172no|	@echo "    INCLUDE_JUDGES=j1,j2 subset of judges for reaggregation"

181hi|	@echo ""

217sv|benchmark-setup:
218sc|	@echo "Ensuring benchmarking configuration exists..."
219ek|	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
220kf|	curl -s -X GET "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)/api/v1/configurations/$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
221ve|		-H "Authorization: Bearer $$TOKEN" | grep -q "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" || \

232eq|# ==============================================================================
233he|# benchmark-study — main entry point (runs inside Docker, idempotent)
234pz|#
235vo|# Starts the benchmark container, then launches run_all_benchmarks.sh inside
236dd|# it with the chosen SUITE.  Each dataset gets a stable RUN_NAME so re-running
237gx|# the same command resumes from where it left off.
238wp|# ==============================================================================
239th|
240si|benchmark-study: benchmark-setup
241sa|	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
242jf|	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
243vw|	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
244ke|	echo "Running suite='$(SUITE)' inside container..."; \
245yj|	INP="$(DATASET)"; [ -z "$$INP" ] && INP="$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)"; \
246sm|	if [ -n "$$INP" ]; then \
247kx|		docker exec \
248ak|			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
249it|			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
250af|			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
251oa|			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
252kd|			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
253cs|			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
254ef|			-e DATASETS_DIR="/dev/null" \
255yi|			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
256ri|			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
257yl|			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
258cx|			benchmark-env \
259tw|			python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
260ff|				--input "$$INP" \
261ej|				--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
262cg|				--output-dir "/app/src/evaluation/results" \
263sv|				$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
264wr|				--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
265jp|				--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
266eg|				--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
267fr|				--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
268az|				--token "$$TOKEN" \
269hd|				--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
270nh|				--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
271ve|				--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
272rh|				$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
273qe|				$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
274ba|				$(if $(ONLY_EVALUATE),--only-evaluate,) \
275iy|				$(if $(ONLY_JUDGE),--only-judge,) \
276tr|				$(if $(SKIP_EVALUATE),--skip-evaluate,) \
277he|				$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
278tc|				$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
279ee|				$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",); \
280jr|	else \
281kx|		docker exec \
282ak|			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
283it|			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
284af|			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
285oa|			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
286kd|			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
287cs|			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
288hv|			-e DATASETS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/datasets" \
289yi|			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
290ri|			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
291yl|			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
292cx|			benchmark-env \
293ts|			bash /app/src/evaluation/run_all_benchmarks.sh; \
294ua|	fi
295th|
296oj|# ==============================================================================
297ei|# benchmark-comparative — single dataset, runs on host (fast iteration)
298so|# ==============================================================================
299th|
300dp|benchmark-comparative: benchmark-setup
301ek|	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
302ln|	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
303gs|		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
304ge|		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
305cu|		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
306zl|		$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
307yf|		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
308kn|		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
309mu|		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
310zp|		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
311br|		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
312mi|		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
